title: Enforce context isolation on every renderer
description: Keep contextIsolation enabled and nodeIntegration disabled so page scripts cannot reach Node or Electron internals directly.
body: Use when creating any BrowserWindow or webview. Do not set nodeIntegration:true or contextIsolation:false to make `require` work in the page, and do not disable the sandbox for convenience. Actions: set contextIsolation:true, nodeIntegration:false, and sandbox:true in webPreferences for every renderer; expose needed capability only through a preload contextBridge. Validation signals include a security audit confirming isolation on all windows and the page being unable to call require or access process. Risk boundary: a renderer with Node access that loads any untrusted or remote content gives an attacker full code execution on the user's machine — disabling isolation is a critical security defect, not a workaround.
